back:
Carotid-cavernous fistula (CCF), see
dilated sup ophthalmic vein
hemangiopericytoma

Clival chordoma: midline, purely lytic on
CT, strong/heterogenous C+

DDx: chondrosarcoma: eccentric/petroclival,
lytic but w internal chondroid matrix on CT, very similar MR characteristics
only *bright on ADC*
Glomus jugulare = paraganglioma

Persistent trigeminal artery (from
cavernous ICA, most common)

Otic = from petrous ICA (very rare, first
anastomosis to regress)

Hyoglossal = from cervical ICA (C1-3), comes
through hypoglossal canal

Proatlantal = from cervical ICA/ECA

Tentorial meningioma fed by artery of bernasconi
& cassinari
(MHT)

Hemangiopericytoma – staghorn vessels, +Vimentin + CD34, -EMA
(unlike meningioma),

Chordoma - Sheets/cords
of large vacuolated cells (physaliphorous
or bubbly cells) surrounded by mucin; +EMA +cytokeratin
(ectoderm) +S100
brachyury (mesenchymal/neural
crest)
Paraganglioma - zellballen

Chondrosarcoma - S100+,
EMA-,
biphasic pattern: spindle shaped cells + well-diff cartilage

front: What structures pass through the jugular foramen?
back:
Internal jugular vein 
Glossopharyngeal nerve
Vagus nerve
Accessory nerve
Inferior petrosal sinus 
Posterior meningeal artery

front: What structures pass through the foramen rotundum?
back: CN V2 (mnemonic: CN rotwondum)

front: What structures pass through the foramen ovale?
back:
CN V3 (mnemonic: ovalee = CN three)

Lesser superficial petrosal nerve

front: What structures pass through the carotid canal?
back:
Sympathetic nerves

ICA

front: What structures pass through Dorello's canal?
back: CN VI: not a true foramen

front: What are the borders and contents of the clinoidal triangle?
back:
Medial: CN II
Lateral: CNIII
Base: Tentorium 
Conents: Clinoidal segment of the ICA

front: What are the borders and contents of the oculomotor (hakuba) triangle?
back:
Medial: interclinoid fold
Lateral: anterior petroclinoid fold
Base: posterior petroclinoid fold 
Contents: CN III

front: What are the borders of the supratrochlear triangle?
back:
Medial: CN III
Lateral: CN IV
Base: Tentorium 
Contents: MHT

front: What are the contents and borders of the anteromedial triangle?
back:
Medial: CN V1
Lateral: CN V2 
Base: Line btwn the SOF and foramen rotundum 
Contents: sphenoid sinus, CN VI

front: What are the contents and borders of the anterolateral triangle?
back:
Medial: CN V2 
Lateral: CN V3 
Base: Line between foramen rotundum and ovale 
Contents: none
Associated with bacterial otitis media . Triad of periorbital/facial pain (CNV), diplopia (CNVI), and persistent otorrhea.

front: What is the Gradenigo syndrome triad?
back:
1) 6th CN palsy
2) retro orbital pain
3) draining ear (caused by otitis media) with mastoiditis and petrous apex involvement
inflammation of superior orbital fissure or cavernous sinus, painful ophthalmoplegia with multiple CN palsies, but pupil is spared; dramatic improvement with steroids

front: What are ocular manifestations of CCF?
back: Ocular manifestations of a carotid cavernous fistula (CCF) include eye redness (conjunctival hyperemia), bulging eye (proptosis), eyelid swelling (ptosis), double vision (diplopia), reduced vision, ophthalmoplegia, and an elevated eye pressure (glaucoma).
